name: Monstera acuminata
aboutText: Monstera acuminata is a tropical wet-forest climber found in the shaded understory and on tree trunks. It thrives in warm, humid environments with persistent moisture and filtered light, native to Mexico and Panama. This hemi-epiphytic climber is characterized by narrow, elongated leaves with oval internal perforations and a thin texture, allowing it to ascend tree trunks and vertical supports with aerial roots.

fieldNotes.title: Pointed and Underappreciated
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A Central American climbing Monstera with acuminate — sharply pointed — leaf tips and moderate fenestration in the adult growth stage. Known from humid forest from Mexico to Panama, it has not attained the cultural profile of its more famous relatives, largely because it lacks a dramatic common name, any particular social media moment, or variegated cultivar to drive demand. A sound, honest plant, growing in the way Monstera are supposed to grow, at a price that reflects its market position rather than its botanical interest.
